title: Tablet VII — The Death of Enkidu
source: CDLI tablet photograph (P395404) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
description: Tablet VII — The Death of Enkidu of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Enkidu's dream of the underworld; his illness and death; Gilgamesh's lament. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P395404). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).
introduction:
This entry presents Tablet VII — The Death of Enkidu of the Epic of Gilgamesh — the oldest major work of literature, composed in Akkadian cuneiform around 2100-1200 BCE.

  1. Tablet photograph: Cuneiform tablet photo from CDLI (artifact P395404), showing the actual clay tablet with the wedge-shaped script.
  2. Original text: Akkadian transliteration from Langdon's scholarly edition (public domain).
  3. English translation: Langdon's English rendering (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

translation.original_text:
_kesda-azag_, a relic, 132, 27.

_ki_, _kin_ for _gim_ = _kima_, 120, 6.

Kidurkazal, daughter of Ninkasi, 145.

translation.original_ref: Tablet VII — The Death of Enkidu, section 2
translation.original_text:
_ki-malla_, to bend. _tig-zu ki-ma-al-la nu-gi-gi_, "Thy neck wearies not in bending," 168, 2. [Correct the translation.]

_ki-in-gin, ki-en-gin_, Sumer, 115, 24; 134, 19; 189, 17.

_KI-SAR, kakkara tasabbit_, 199, 29.

_kurgal_, "great mountain," title of Sumer, 114, 11. Of Enlil, 114, 19; 182, 5.
